title: Management Summary — Architectural Anti-Patterns fragment
description: Structured data for the optional '### Architectural Anti-Patterns' callout in the Management Summary. Names the recognized design-level defects the report already articulates in §7 prose, so they are visible at executive level instead of buried in per-control narrative. Authored by the threat-renderer ONLY when at least one genuine architectural anti-pattern is present; omit the file entirely when none apply (the section then renders nothing).

anti_patterns.description: Named architectural anti-patterns, most severe first. Each is a structural design defect that spans multiple findings and cannot be closed by a point fix.

name.description: Canonical anti-pattern name — e.g. 'SPA without BFF', 'JWT in localStorage', 'Raw SQL string interpolation', 'Secrets hardcoded in source', 'Missing server-side authorization layer', 'Sanitizer bypass by default'. A named pattern, NOT a generic domain ('Cryptography') or a finding restatement.

severity.description: Optional traffic-light cue driving the leading emoji. Defaults to red (architectural anti-patterns are structural by nature). Use yellow only for a partially-mitigated pattern.

description.description: 1-2 sentence design-review prose: what structural boundary is defeated and why a single-line patch will not close it. References components/files GENERICALLY (no line-by-line SAST listing — that lives in the linked findings and §7).

affected_components.description: Component-ids (or {id, name} dicts) carrying the pattern. The renderer enriches bare ids to '[C-NN](#c-nn) — Name'.

findings.description: 1-4 representative findings the pattern manifests in. Each {ref, label}; renderer emits '[ref](#anchor) — label'.
